sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Intersect governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, and critical process management for June 2026 to June 2027.
Recipient: Intersect via the 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v

Deliverables: WP 1 - Intersect operations and ecosystem coordination: 6,000,000 ADA, WP 2 - Technical Stewardship, Incident Response & Coordination, including core Cardano repos: 18,800,000 ADA, WP 3 - Management of critical processes: 600,000 ADA

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect states the proposal funds its core operating model for June 2026 to June 2027, including governance coordination, reserved capacity for critical unowned processes, technical stewardship, incident response, and release coordination.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect states the total ask is 25,400,000 ADA, split into 6,000,000 ADA for operations and ecosystem coordination, 18,800,000 ADA for technical stewardship and incident response, and 600,000 ADA for management of critical processes.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Intersect asserts the current proposal is more focused than last year and reduces the overall ask from $7.875M to $6.35M while preserving critical Cardano continuity functions.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Intersect states it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Intersect claims the proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and provides external references to voting documentation and audited results.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Intersect claims the requested amount does not, at submission, individually or in aggregate breach the applicable 350M ADA Net Change Limit for Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 and links to the relevant governance action.
missing: Independent evidence for: Intersect asserts the current proposal is more focused than last year and reduces the overall ask from $7.875M to $6.35M while preserving critical Cardano continuity functions.
Independent evidence for: Intersect states it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Intersect states the proposal funds its core operating model for June 2026 to June 2027, including governance coordination, reserved capacity for critical unowned processes, technical stewardship, incident response, and release coordination." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Intersect asserts the current proposal is more focused than last year and reduces the overall ask from $7.875M to $6.35M while preserving critical Cardano continuity functions." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
